# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Request, Response } from "https://deno.land/x/lambda@1.0.0";
import { permissions } from "https://deno.land/std/permissions/mod.ts";

export async function handleRequest(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = request.queryStringParameters["url"];

  if (!url) {
    return {
      statusCode: 400,
      body: JSON.stringify({ error: "Missing URL query parameter" }),
    };
  }

  try {
    const permission = await permissions.request("net");

    if (permission !== true) {
      return {
        statusCode: 403,
        body: JSON.stringify({ error: "Permission denied for network access" }),
      };
    }

    const response = await fetch(url);
    const content = await response.text();

    return {
      statusCode: 200,
      body: JSON.stringify({ content }),
    };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error fetching data from URL", error);
    return {
      statusCode: 500,
      body: JSON.stringify({ error: "Internal server error" }),
    };
  }
}
